title: The One With All The Resolutions

lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, Monica and Rachel are hosting a New Year's party. So the place is crowded and in a shameless promotion for NBC they're watching Jay Leno's coverage of New Year's from Time Square.

lines.speaker: Jay Leno
lines.text: (On TV to Dick Clark) Is there any entertainment there? What are people doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! Here we go! 1999! The year of Joey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (deadpan, standing next to Monica) We're very happy for you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's the matter?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We wanted to kiss at midnight, but nobody else is going to so y'know...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I'll take care of it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no, wait! Joey!
They try to stop him, not sure of what he's planning. He ignores them and goes to talk to Ross.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross! Ross! Ross, listen! Who are you kissing at midnight, huh? Rachel or Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well you gotta kiss someone, you can't kiss your sister.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, who's gonna kiss my sister.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Awww, man! Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude-dude, who would you rather have kiss your sister, me or Chandler?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's a good point.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh well, since I have that whole history with Rachel, I guess Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs! Pheebs! Ross wants to kiss you at midnight!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's so obvious, why doesn't he just ask?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach! Rach! Listen, I'm gonna kiss you at midnight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, everyone's gotta kiss someone. You can't kiss Ross you got the history.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So? Who would you rather have kiss you, me or Chandler?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, good point.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: (watching the ball drop) 3! 2! 1! HAPPY NEW YEAR!!
And with that everyone starts playing tonsil hockey. Chandler with Monica, Ross with Phoebe, and Joey with Rachel.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) Happy New Year!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Happy New Year.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Happy New Year, Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You too!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Joey) Happy New Year, Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So did that do anything for ya?
Rachel slowly walks away.
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, after the party. Everyone has left, except for the gang.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what? I'm gonna go out on a limb and say no divorces in '99!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But your divorce isn't even final yet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just the one divorce in '99! Y'know what, I am gonna be happy this year. I am gonna make myself happy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you want us to leave the room, or?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Everyday I am gonna do one thing that I haven't done before. That my friends is my New Year's resolution.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh! That's a good one! Mine is to pilot a commercial jet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's good one too, Pheebs. Now all you have to do is find a planeload of people who's resolution is to plummet to their deaths.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe your resolution is to not make fun of your friends, especially the ones who may soon be flying you to Europe for free on their own plane.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She has a better chance of sprouting wings and flying up your nose than you do of not making fun of us.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: In fact, I'll bet you 50 bucks that you can't go the whole year without making fun of us. Eh, y'know what, better yet? A week.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll take that bet my friend. And you know what, paying me the 50 bucks could be the "new thing you do that day!" (Ross looks at him.) And it starts right now!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, my New Year's resolution is to learn how to play the guitar.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?! How come?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, y'know those special skills I have listed on my resume? I would love it would be great if one of those was true.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Do you want me to teach you? I'm a great teacher.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? Who-who have you taught?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I taught me and I love me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah that'd be great! Thanks Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Op, look! Claire forgot her glasses! And she's gonna be really needing these to keep an eye on that boyfriend, who, I hear, needs to keep his stapler in his desk drawer, if you know what I'm talking about.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Rach, maybe your resolution should be to umm, gossip less.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't gossip!
They all laugh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, maybe sometimes I find out things or I hear something and I pass that information on y'know kinda like a public service, it doesn't mean I'm a gossip. I mean, would you call Ted Kopel a gossip?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well if Ted Kopel talked about his coworkers botched boob jobs, I would.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? They were like this!
She puts her hands over her breasts and indicates that the coworkers boob job resulted in one pointing up and one pointing down with her hands by pointing up with one hand and down with the other.
Central Perk, Ross is talking to a beautiful woman as Chandler and Joey enter.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I'll see you tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay! (She leaves.) (To Chandler and Joey.) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just asked that girl out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nice!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nice!! Yeah! Is that part of your resolution, your new thing for today?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes it is. See. (Shows them the piece of paper she gave him with her name and phone number on it.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (reading it) Elizabeth Hornswoggle?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right, uh, Elizabeth Hornswoggle.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Horn-swoggle.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You all right Chandler? Is there something funny about that name?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. No, I just think that maybe I-I'd heard it somewhere before. (Sits down rubbing his temple.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh really! Where? Somewhere funny I'd bet! (Chandler is straining to keep quiet as Phoebe enters.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi, Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-oh, guess what? I-I have a date with Elizabeth (Talking into Chandler's ear.) Hornswoggle.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hornswoggle? (To Chandler) Ooh, this must be killing you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, see you later.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: See ya! All right Pheebs, I am ready for my first lesson.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Joey tries to take the guitar.) Oh no-no-no, you don't touch the guitar! First you learn here, (Points to her head.) then you learn here. (Points to the guitar.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Umm, okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, lesson one: chords. Now, I don't know the actual names of the chords but umm, I-I-I made up names for the way my hand looks while I'm doing them. (She starts to show Joey the chords. Transcribers Note: For this one you'll have to use your imagination, 'cause it would take me 50 pages to describe each one. So if you want to see them, you'll have to wait for this episode to come to a TV near you.) (Holding up her hand and then reconfiguring her hand with each name.) So then, this is Bear Claw. Okay, umm, Turkey Leg and Old Lady. (Joey tries to imitate them.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What an interesting approach to guitar instruction. Y'know some might find it amusing, I myself find it regular.
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is entering with Rachel.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey everybody, Rachel was so good today. She didn't gossip at all.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I didn't! Even when I found out...umm, all right, well let's just say I found something out something about someone and let's just say she's gonna keep it. (Goes into her room.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Pheebs! Check-check this out. (Holds up his hand in one of Phoebe's chords.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, you nailed the Old Lady! (They both laugh at what she said.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah listen so, I thought I was getting better, so on my way home today I stopped by this guitar store and...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Did you, did you touch any of the guitars while you were there? Did you?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Give me your hands. (He does and she smells his left hand.) Strings. Gimme it! (He gives her his right hand and she smells it as well.) Pick. Do you want to learn to play guitar?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Then don't touch one!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering, with Ben) Hi!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi Ben!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Auntie Monica!! (He runs to hug her.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (notices something) Ross is wearing leather pants! Does nobody else see that Ross is wearing leather pants? (Pause, no one speaks.) Someone comment on the pants!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I think they're very nice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I like 'em.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! (Chandler bangs the table in frustration.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I like them a lot.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's not what I had in mind! See, people like Ross don't generally wear these types of pants. You see, they're very tight. (Motions to Ross's buttock.) Maybe there's something in that area.
They all speak at once in general approval of his pants selection; Joey asks where he got them. I can't pick out the rest of it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh see, I-I needed a new thing for today and there's this leather store that always smells so good and I thought to myself, "Wow, (To Chandler) I never really owned a good smelling pair of pants before."
No one says anything.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on!! (Storms out.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (after he's left) Okay, seriously, what do you think?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You look like a freak.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Awful, absolutely awful. (Plus other negative comments from Phoebe and Monica.)
Monica starts taking pictures of Ross and Ben, with the flash.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's my New Year's resolution!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, to blind my child?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! To take more pictures of all of us together. I mean I really think it's the best resolution because everyone will enjoy the pictures.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, everyone will enjoy my music as well. (Does a chord and Monica starts taking pictures of him and Phoebe. They both start to strike a pose with the excessive amount of pictures that Monica takes.)
Elizabeth Hornswoggle's apartment; Ross is there on his date with her. They are sitting on the couch watching a movie. Ross is obviously hot.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (talking to himself) My God! These pants are burning up! (He's still wearing the leather pants.) (She snuggles closer.) Oh come on, she wants to snuggle now! What is she trying to kill me? It's like a volcano in here! (Out loud.) Are you hot?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth Hornswoggle
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, it must just be me then.

He shifts on the couch and a ripping sound emanates from his lower regions, the sound reminds one of a brief explosion of gas. In other words, it sounds like he farted. She has a look of horrific wonderment, wondering "Did he just fart?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That was just the pants on the couch. Umm, hey, do you, do you mind if I use your bathroom?

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: No, go ahead.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks. (Gets up and as he does so, the sound returns. Without another word he heads into her bathroom.)

[Cut to Elizabeth Hornswoggle's bathroom, Ross frantically pulls his shirt out and drops his pants. He exhales in sheer ecstasy as the coolness of the bathroom envelops his legs. He sits on the cast iron bathtub, again gasping in pleasure. He next grabs a magazine and starts to blow air on his exposed legs, but that doesn't work the way he wants it to. So he throws the magazine down, looks around for another idea, and finds one. He jumps up and hops to the sink. He turns on the water and starts to splash some on his legs, cooling them further.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (in ecstasy) Ahhhhhhhhhhhhhh..........

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: (yelling from outside) Hey, my favorite part is coming up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 'Kay!
He goes to pull up his pants, but can't seem to get them past his knees. He frantically tries to pull them up to no avail. Panic sets in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God!
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is drilling Joey on the chords as Monica looks on.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Tiger! (Joey moves his hand in an attempt to do the chord, in fact he does it with each name.) Dragon! Iceberg!! (He fails.) Joseph, did you even study at all last night?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! Yes, I did.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Then do Iceberg!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (thinks) (quietly) G-sharp.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: G-sharp? Have you been studying the real names of the chords? (Joey doesn't answer.) Have you? (He looks away in shame.) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! I didn't touch a guitar!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, but you're questioning my method!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I'm not questioning it, I'm saying it's stupid! (Notices Monica standing between them and smiling.) What?! (The camera clicks, taking another picture.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you. (Leaves.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know none of my other student thought I was stupid.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Your other student, was you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, well, y'know maybe you just need to try a little harder!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looking in the Yellow Pages) Look, maybe I need to try a real teacher! Right here! Here! Andy Cooper, he teaches guitar and look ooh, there's a nice picture of him with a little kid and THE KID'S GOT A GUITAR!!!!!! (Storms out.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fine! You go learn from your qualified instructor! But don't come crying to me when everyone's sick and tired of hearing you play Bad, Bad Leroy Brown!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering, singing) "Baddest man in the whole damn town."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, fine! Take his side! (Storms out.)

Rachel looks shocked, gets over it, notices she's alone, and picks up the phone. The last part of which is something she shouldn't have done, because the phone is already in use. By Monica, and now Rachel can hear every word.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (on phone) I can't wait to be with you! I'll sneak over as soon as Ross picks up Ben. I'll just tell Rachel I'm gonna be doing laundry for a couple of hours.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (on phone) Laundry. Huh. Is that my new nickname? (Rachel is absolutely stunned, she opens her mouth in absolute amazement.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (on phone) Awww, y'know what your nickname is, Mr. Big...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Arghh!! (She quickly hangs up the phone and starts to pace around wondering what to do.)
Commercial Break
Chandler, Joey, and Ross's, The phone rings and Joey answers it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (answering phone) Hello?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (calling from Elizabeth's bathroom) Joey, it's Ross! I need some help!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, Chandler's not here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, you can help me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen, I'm in Elizabeth's bathroom...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nice!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I-I got really hot in my leather pants so I took them off but they must have shrunk from the-the sweat or-or-or my legs expanded from the heat. Look, I-I can't put them back on. I can't!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. That is quite a situation. Uh, do you see any like, powder?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Powder! Yeah! Yeah, I have powder! (Grabs some of her shelf.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good-good, okay, sprinkle some of that on your legs, it'll absorb some of the moisture and then you can get your pants back up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, okay, hold on! (He puts the phone down and proceeds to spread a large amount of powder on his legs and makes another attempt at pulling up his pants. It doesn't work, and without picking up the phone leans down to it.) (Almost in tears.) They're not coming on man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Umm, do you see any-oh, Vaseline?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh, I-I see lotion, I have lotion! Will that work?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, sure, spread some of that on there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hold on.

Ross proceeds to apply copious amounts of the lotion on his legs. He literally starts spraying the back of his legs with the lotion, and as he applies some to his butt he makes a happy face like he enjoyed that sensation. After using about half the bottle he again tries to pull up his pants, but at the first sign of resistance, his hand slips off of the pants and hits him in the forehead.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross? You okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They're still, they're still not coming on man and the lotion and the powder have made a paste!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?! Uhh, what color is it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What difference does that make?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I'm just-if the paste matches the pants, you can make yourself a pair of paste pants and she won't know the difference!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Joey, do you have a minute?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dude, what am I gonna...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Ross) Uh, Rachel's here, so good luck man, let me know how it works out. (He hangs up the phone and strands Ross in the bathroom.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Joey, I have such a problem!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well, you're timing couldn't be better. I am putting out fires all over the place.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Okay. Okay. Joey, I have got to tell you something!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What-what is it, what is it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, it's so huge, but you just have to promise me that you cannot tell anyone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no, no-no-no-no! I don't want to know!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! Yes! Yes, you do want to know! This is unbelievable!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't care, Rach! Look, I am tired of being the guy who knows all the secrets but can't tell anyone!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? What secrets? You know secrets? What are they?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And you're not supposed to be gossiping!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, I know! I just can't keep this one in, so I pick up the phone... (Joey in a childish attempt to not hear what Rachel is about to say, puts his fingers in his ears and starts to scream loudly. Rachel turns and walks out upon seeing that Joey's not gonna listen, and as she exits Chandler walks in and sees Joey in his current state.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm not listening to you!

Chandler seeing that Joey has his eyes closed sneaks over and picks up the chicken. The chicken starts flapping it's wings in protest as Chandler holds the chicken inches from Joey's face. Joey stops yelling and upon opening his eyes sees the chicken, screams, and falls to the ground in horror.
Elizabeth's apartment; Elizabeth is inquiring as to the delay in Ross's exit from her bathroom.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Ross, umm, you've been in there for a long time. I'm starting to get kinda freaked out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, I'm coming out. Hey, can you turn the lights off.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: No, let's just leave the lights on.

Ross opens the door and steps into the living room. He has fully removed his pants and holds them wrapped into a ball in front of his crotch. His legs are covered in the powder and lotion paste. He looks terrible.

lines.speaker: Elizabeth
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I had a problem.
Central Perk, Joey is there to apologize to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I can't talk to you! I don't have a fancy ad in the Yellow Pages!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, Pheebs, I just, I wanna apologize for, for saying that your method was stupid and-and maybe ask you to be my teacher again. And-and I promise, I won't touch a guitar until you say I'm ready. (After a short pause she hands him her guitar.) You really think I'm ready?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! Cool! (He takes the guitar, stands up, and goes to play a note. However, while strumming it, he knocks it out of his hands and it bounces off the table and lands on the floor. Phoebe just stares at the guitar.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sitting back down) Was the chord at least right...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No!
Monica and Rachel's, Ross is returning from his horrible trek back home without pants on. The whole gang is there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We heard about your pants, I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This year was supposed to be great! But, it's only the second day and I'm a loser with stupid leather pants that don't even fit!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: No. No, you're not a loser.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look at me! (Chandler squeaks in an attempt not to make fun of him.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, hey, look. Look Ross, Ben drew a picture of you! (Shows him Ben's picture.) Huh? You're-you're a cowboy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, be-because of the leather pants.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: See? Ben doesn't think you're a loser, he thinks you're a cowboy! Now that's something.
All at once.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That really is something; that's really cool.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Howdy partner!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Maybe I should get another pair! Ooh, y'know, they-they had some with fringe all down the sides. (Chandler starts rubbing his temple again.) I'm gonna go kiss Ben goodnight. (He starts to head for Monica's bedroom.) I can't believe he thinks I'm a cowboy. (Pause.) I would make a good cowboy. (He struts into Monica's room.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, now that everything's wrapped up here, I think I'm, I'm gonna go do my laundry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, me too. Y'know if this shirt is dirty. (Smells it.) Yep.
They both exit.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I'm gonna go too. I'm gonna go to the airport. I figure if I hang around there long enough, someone's bound to leave one of those planes unattended.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good luck, honey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Bye! (She exits, leaving Rachel and Joey alone.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, uh, Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Umm?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Remember that big thing I was gonna tell you about?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, no! (Starts that screaming thing again. Rachel stops him by pulling his fingers out of his ears.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm not gonna tell you, but if you found out on your own, that would be okay and then we could talk about it. Right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, then it wouldn't be a secret. So yeah, that would be okay. Yeah. Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (quietly) Yeah. Well. (Pause.) Hey uh Joe, would mind going over to Chandler's bedroom and get that book back that he borrowed from me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now? You want me to go over there now?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do you know something?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you know something?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I might know something.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I might know something too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's the thing you know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, I can't tell you until you tell me what you know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't tell you what I know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then I can't tell you what I know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, fine.
Silence ensues.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You don't know!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, how about I go over there and I will walk into Chandler's bedroom and I will see that thing that I think that I know is actually the thing that I think that I know! (Note: Kudos to Ms. Aniston on the delivery of that line. She said it very quickly and didn't screw up a word. Try it yourself, it ain't that easy.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (gasps) YOU KNOW!!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: AND YOU KNOW!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I know!!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler and Monica?!! Oh, this is unbelievable!! How long have you known?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Too long! Oh my God, Rach, I've been dying to talk to someone about this for so long! Listen, listen, we can't say anything about this to anybody, they're so weird about that! Listen... (Phoebe returns and interrupts them.)

lines.speaker: Joey and Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! It's raining. I don't want to fly in the rain. So...
Pause.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I am going to go for a walk in the rain.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhh, yeah, me too.
They both exit.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's weird. (Pause.) I bet they're doing it.
Ending Credits
Central Perk, Phoebe, Ross, and Monica are there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Oh good, okay, I can't take it anymore. I can't take it anymore. So you win, okay? Here! (Hands him the 50 bucks he's about to owe him.) Pheebs? Flying a jet? Better make it a spaceship so that you can get back to your home planet! And Ross, phone call for you today, Tom Jones, he wants his pants back! And Hornswoggle? What are you dating a character from Fraggile Rock?! (He sits down and sighs in relief.)
End
